1 # Step 7 — DHH v4 final (2026-04-27) 2 3 > **Supersedes v3** ([08-dhh-v3-final-merged-2026-04-27.md](08-dhh-v3-final-merged-2026-04-27.md)). Synthesised from QA pass 3 + Elon first-principles pass 3. 4 > 5 > v4 is the executable plan. Same overall scope as v3 (Wave 1 + unified Wave 2). Adds: token-digest pattern for cookie security, deferred User creation on marketing pages, email normalisation, validation tightening, encryption-at-rest test, and 5-commit packaging for Wave 2. 6 7 ## Changes from v3 8 9 **Adopted (folded in)**: 10 - Q1: `User.token` → `User.token_digest` for cookie security. Cookie carries plaintext, DB stores hash. 11 - Q2: `Mobile::BaseController#ensure_user!` removed; `requires_user!` filter only on actions that need it (onboarding, auth). Marketing pages don't create User rows. 12 - Q3: `User#normalise_email_address` callback (downcase + strip before save) — works correctly with deterministic encryption. 13 - Q4: `numericality` validation on `times_divorced` / `times_widowed` (≥ 0). 14 - Q6: Migration ordering note rewritten as "atomic deploy." 15 - Q7: `User#first_incomplete_path` and `welcome_complete?`/`location_complete?`/etc. inlined explicitly. 16 - Q8: Server-side login confirmation as backup to Stimulus warning. 17 - Q11: Encryption-at-rest test. 18 - Q12: Cookie cutover test at registration. 19 - Elon E5: Wave 2 packaged as 5 atomic commits, not 12. 20 21 **Adjusted with Q2 in mind**: 22 - Will-from-day-one preserved. With Q2 in place ("only create User on meaningful action"), Wills are created for engaged users only — not for marketing-page visitors. Acceptable. 23 24 **Explicitly rejected**: 25 - Elon E2 ("don't materialize anything until welcome step"): too disruptive vs the Q2 partial fix. Q2 captures most of the benefit (no User on marketing pages); going further requires rebuilding the attribution capture mechanism. Defer to a future plan if bot traffic actually becomes a concern. 26 - Elon E3 (drop Will-from-day-one): the user explicitly confirmed Will-from-day-one. With Q2, the cost is negligible. 27 - Q10 (filter person-picker dropdown): keeping the long list. Epic 4 person-merge feature handles cleanup. 28 - Q13 (cleanup job load test): deferred to PRE_LAUNCH_TODO. 29 - Q14, Q15, Q16, Q17 nits: deferred. 30 31 ## Note to AI agent 32 33 Forbidden: 34 - No service objects (`app/services/` is off limits). 35 - No form objects, presenter objects, "policy" objects. 36 - No new gems beyond what's already in the Gemfile. 37 - No premature extraction of helpers, concerns, or shared modules until at least three duplicates exist. 38 - No defensive code for cases that can't happen. 39 - No rescuing exceptions you don't have a specific recovery for. 40 - Multi-row write logic lives on the relevant Active Record model as fat-model methods, not in service-object classes. 41 - No default scopes (DHH dislikes them; explicit > implicit). 42 43 --- 44 45 ## Wave structure (unchanged) 46 47 - **Wave 1**: 1 day. Internal-testing unblock. **Identical to v3 / v2.** See [v2 §Wave 1](06-dhh-v2-final-2026-04-27.md#wave-1) for full detail. 48 - **Wave 2**: ~10 days. Five atomic commits, ships in a single deploy. 49 50 --- 51 52 ## Wave 2 — commit packaging 53 54 5 commits, ordered. Each is a logically coherent atomic unit. CI runs each commit + the chain. 55 56 ### Commit 1 — Schema + encryption (~3 days) 57 58 **Files**: 8 migrations, encryption setup. No code change visible to users. 59 60 #### 1A. Migrations 61 62 Eight migrations, run in numbered order on a fresh DB. **Single deploy: all migrations + code refactor in subsequent commits land together.** 63 64 ```ruby 65 # db/migrate/20260427120001_make_user_auth_fields_nullable.rb 66 class MakeUserAuthFieldsNullable < ActiveRecord::Migration[8.1] 67 def change 68 change_column_null :users, :email_address, true 69 change_column_null :users, :password_digest, true 70 remove_index :users, :email_address 71 add_index :users, :email_address, 72 unique: true, 73 where: "email_address IS NOT NULL", 74 name: "index_users_on_email_address_when_set" 75 end 76 end 77 ``` 78 79 ```ruby 80 # db/migrate/20260427120002_add_anonymous_lifecycle_to_users.rb 81 class AddAnonymousLifecycleToUsers < ActiveRecord::Migration[8.1] 82 def change 83 # Q1 fix: token_digest, NOT plaintext token 84 add_column :users, :token_digest, :string 85 add_index :users, :token_digest, unique: true 86 87 add_column :users, :current_step, :string 88 add_column :users, :intro_seen_at, :datetime 89 add_column :users, :last_seen_at, :datetime 90 add_column :users, :completed_at, :datetime 91 92 add_column :users, :attribution_source, :string 93 add_column :users, :campaign, :string 94 add_column :users, :raw_url, :text 95 add_column :users, :first_show, :string 96 add_column :users, :video_intro_version, :integer 97 add_column :users, :risk_questionnaire_version, :integer 98 add_column :users, :video_completed_at, :datetime 99 add_column :users, :questionnaire_completed_at, :datetime 100 end 101 end 102 ``` 103 104 Migrations 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 are **identical to v3** (`CreatePeople`, `AddWillMakerPersonIdToUsers`, `CreateParentages`, `CreateMarriages`, `CreateWills`, `DropOnboardingSessionsTable`). See [v3 W2.1](08-dhh-v3-final-merged-2026-04-27.md#w21--schema-migrations) for those exact migrations. 105 106 #### 1B. Encryption setup 107 108 ```bash 109 bin/rails db:encryption:init 110 ``` 111 112 Add the generated keys to `config/credentials/{development,test,production}.yml.enc`. 113 114 #### 1C. Tests landing in Commit 1 115 116 ```ruby 117 # spec/models/encryption_at_rest_spec.rb (Q11) 118 RSpec.describe "PII encryption at rest" do 119 it "stores Person.first_name as ciphertext, never plaintext" do 120 user = User.create! 121 person = user.people.create!(relationship_kind: "self", first_name: "Alice") 122 123 raw = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.exec_query( 124 "SELECT first_name FROM people WHERE id = #{person.id}" 125 ).first["first_name"] 126 127 expect(raw).not_to eq "Alice" 128 expect(raw.length).to be > 30 129 expect(person.first_name).to eq "Alice" 130 end 131 132 it "stores User.email_address as ciphertext" do 133 user = User.create!(email_address: "test@example.com", password: "securepass1234") 134 raw = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.exec_query( 135 "SELECT email_address FROM users WHERE id = #{user.id}" 136 ).first["email_address"] 137 expect(raw).not_to eq "test@example.com" 138 end 139 end 140 ``` 141 142 ### Commit 2 — User model + BaseController + cleanup job (~2.5 days) 143 144 **Files**: `app/models/user.rb`, `app/controllers/mobile/base_controller.rb`, `app/jobs/abandoned_anonymous_user_cleanup_job.rb`, schedule update for solid_queue. 145 146 #### 2A. User model 147 148 ```ruby 149 class User < ApplicationRecord 150 has_secure_password validations: false 151 152 has_many :sessions, dependent: :destroy 153 has_many :api_sessions, dependent: :destroy 154 has_many :devices, dependent: :destroy 155 156 has_many :people, dependent: :destroy 157 belongs_to :will_maker_person, class_name: "Person", optional: true 158 has_one :spouse_person, -> { where(relationship_kind: "spouse") }, 159 class_name: "Person", inverse_of: :user 160 161 has_many :wills, dependent: :destroy 162 has_one :active_will, -> { where(status: "active") }, class_name: "Will" 163 has_one :draft_will, -> { where(status: "draft") }, class_name: "Will" 164 165 encrypts :email_address, deterministic: true 166 167 validates :email_address, 168 uniqueness: { case_sensitive: false }, 169 presence: true, 170 on: :register 171 validates :password, 172 length: { minimum: 12 }, 173 format: { with: /\A(?=.*[a-zA-Z])(?=.*[0-9]).*\z/, message: "must contain letters and numbers" }, 174 on: :register 175 176 # Q3: normalise email before encryption to match deterministic encryption uniqueness 177 before_save :normalise_email_address 178 179 # Will-from-day-one 180 after_create :create_initial_will 181 182 attr_accessor :token # Q1: transient — DB stores token_digest 183 before_create :assign_token 184 185 # === State predicates === 186 187 def anonymous? 188 email_address.nil? 189 end 190 191 def registered? 192 email_address.present? 193 end 194 195 # === Auth === 196 197 def self.authenticate_by(email_address:, password:) 198 return nil if email_address.blank? || password.blank? 199 normalised = email_address.downcase.strip 200 user = where.not(email_address: nil).find_by(email_address: normalised) 201 return nil if user.nil? || user.password_digest.blank? 202 user.authenticate(password) ? user : nil 203 end 204 205 def self.find_by_cookie_token(plaintext) 206 return nil if plaintext.blank? 207 digest = Digest::SHA256.hexdigest(plaintext) 208 find_by(token_digest: digest, email_address: nil) 209 end 210 211 # === Onboarding step predicates (Q7 inlined) === 212 213 def first_incomplete_path 214 return Rails.application.routes.url_helpers.mobile_onboarding_welcome_path unless welcome_complete? 215 return Rails.application.routes.url_helpers.mobile_onboarding_location_path unless location_complete? 216 return Rails.application.routes.url_helpers.mobile_onboarding_family_path unless family_complete? 217 return Rails.application.routes.url_helpers.mobile_onboarding_extended_family_path unless extended_family_complete? 218 Rails.application.routes.url_helpers.mobile_onboarding_wrap_up_path 219 end 220 221 def welcome_complete? 222 will_maker_person.present? && 223 will_maker_person.first_name.present? && 224 will_maker_person.last_name.present? && 225 will_maker_person.date_of_birth.present? 226 end 227 228 def location_complete? 229 will_maker_person.present? && 230 will_maker_person.country_of_residence.present? && 231 will_maker_person.nationality.present? && 232 will_maker_person.domiciled_in_uk.present? && 233 will_maker_person.currently_resident_in_uk.present? 234 end 235 236 def family_complete? 237 current_step.in?(%w[extended_family wrap_up]) || completed_at.present? 238 end 239 240 def extended_family_complete? 241 current_step == "wrap_up" || completed_at.present? 242 end 243 244 # === Lifecycle === 245 246 def inactive_for_more_than?(duration) 247 last_seen_at && last_seen_at < duration.ago 248 end 249 250 def touch_last_seen! 251 update_column(:last_seen_at, Time.current) 252 end 253 254 # === Onboarding step writes (fat-model methods) === 255 # apply_welcome_step!, apply_location_step!, apply_family_step!, 256 # apply_extended_family_step!, apply_partner!, apply_children!, apply_co_parent!, 257 # destroy_existing_partnership!, destroy_orphaned_co_parents! 258 # Implementation identical to v2 [§W2.5]; just operates on `self.people` not `onboarding_session.people`. 259 260 private 261 262 def normalise_email_address 263 self.email_address = email_address.downcase.strip if email_address.present? 264 end 265 266 def assign_token 267 self.token = SecureRandom.hex(32) 268 self.token_digest = Digest::SHA256.hexdigest(token) 269 end 270 271 def create_initial_will 272 wills.create!(version: 1, status: "draft") 273 end 274 end 275 ``` 276 277 Plus `Person` model adds `numericality` validation: 278 ```ruby 279 validates :times_divorced, :times_widowed, 280 numericality: { greater_than_or_equal_to: 0, only_integer: true } 281 ``` 282 283 #### 2B. BaseController (Q2 fix folded in) 284 285 ```ruby 286 class Mobile::BaseController < ApplicationController 287 helper_method :current_user 288 289 before_action :touch_user_if_present 290 291 private 292 293 def current_user 294 @current_user ||= find_current_user 295 end 296 297 def find_current_user 298 if user_id = session[:user_id] 299 registered = User.find_by(id: user_id) 300 return registered if registered 301 session.delete(:user_id) # stale; clear 302 end 303 304 if cookie_token = cookies.signed[:user_token] 305 anonymous = User.find_by_cookie_token(cookie_token) 306 return anonymous if anonymous && !stale?(anonymous) 307 cleanup_stale_user!(anonymous, cookie_token) if anonymous 308 end 309 310 nil 311 end 312 313 # Only call from controllers that need a User (onboarding, auth). 314 # Marketing pages (intro, video-intro, risk-questionnaire) do NOT create Users. 315 def requires_user! 316 @current_user = current_user || create_anonymous_user! 317 end 318 319 def create_anonymous_user! 320 user = User.create! 321 cookies.signed[:user_token] = { 322 value: user.token, # transient plaintext 323 expires: 3.hours.from_now, 324 httponly: true, 325 same_site: :lax, 326 secure: Rails.env.production? 327 } 328 user 329 end 330 331 def stale?(user) 332 user.last_seen_at.present? && user.last_seen_at < 3.hours.ago 333 end 334 335 def cleanup_stale_user!(stale_user, cookie_token) 336 stale_user.destroy 337 cookies.delete :user_token 338 @current_user = nil 339 end 340 341 def touch_user_if_present 342 current_user&.touch_last_seen! if current_user&.anonymous? 343 end 344 345 def authenticate_user! 346 return if current_user&.registered? 347 redirect_to mobile_login_path 348 end 349 end 350 ``` 351 352 #### 2C. Controllers that require_user! 353 354 ```ruby 355 class Mobile::OnboardingController < Mobile::BaseController 356 before_action :requires_user! 357 # ... (W2.5 logic, calling current_user.apply_*_step!) 358 end 359 360 class Mobile::AuthController < Mobile::BaseController 361 before_action :requires_user! 362 # ... (W2.7 logic) 363 end 364 ``` 365 366 `Mobile::StartupController` (which renders /mobile, /mobile/intro, /mobile/video-intro, /mobile/risk-questionnaire) does **NOT** call `requires_user!`. These are visible to bots without creating User rows. 367 368 When the user clicks "Continue" on the intro step (which POSTs to `/mobile/intro/continue`), the receiving action calls `requires_user!` — User created at that moment, attribution captured, redirect to first onboarding step. 369 370 #### 2D. Cleanup job 371 372 ```ruby 373 class AbandonedAnonymousUserCleanupJob < ApplicationJob 374 queue_as :default 375 376 def perform 377 User.where(email_address: nil) 378 .where("last_seen_at < ? OR (last_seen_at IS NULL AND created_at < ?)", 3.hours.ago, 3.hours.ago) 379 .find_each(batch_size: 100, &:destroy) 380 end 381 end 382 ``` 383 384 Schedule via Solid Queue cron (or whatever scheduler is in use) — runs hourly. 385 386 ### Commit 3 — Onboarding controller + family-step UX (~3 days) 387 388 **Files**: `app/controllers/mobile/onboarding_controller.rb`, `app/views/mobile/onboarding/*`, `app/javascript/controllers/family_form_controller.js`. 389 390 #### 3A. OnboardingController refactor 391 392 Replace every `onboarding_session` reference with `current_user`. Per-action logic: 393 394 ```ruby 395 class Mobile::OnboardingController < Mobile::BaseController 396 before_action :requires_user! 397 398 def update_welcome 399 current_user.apply_welcome_step!(welcome_params) 400 redirect_to mobile_onboarding_location_path 401 rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e 402 @errors = e.record.errors 403 render :welcome, status: :unprocessable_entity 404 end 405 406 # update_location, update_family, update_extended_family, etc. follow the same pattern. 407 408 private 409 410 def welcome_params 411 params.require(:person).permit(:first_name, :middle_names, :last_name, :date_of_birth) 412 end 413 414 def family_params 415 params.permit( 416 :relationship_kind, :marriage_kind, :times_divorced, :times_widowed, :has_children, 417 partner: [:first_name, :last_name, :started_at], 418 children: [ 419 :first_name, :last_name, :date_of_birth, :relationship_kind, 420 :disabled, :lacks_mental_capacity, 421 co_parent: [:type, :existing_person_id, :first_name, :last_name, :relationship_to_child] 422 ] 423 ) 424 end 425 end 426 ``` 427 428 #### 3B. Family-step UX expansion 429 430 Per-child co-parent question — see [v3 W2.8](08-dhh-v3-final-merged-2026-04-27.md#w28--family-step-ux-expansion) for full template + Stimulus controller. 431 432 Changes from v3 plan: 433 - Submit button: `data: { disable_with: "Saving..." }` (B9 fix from QA pass 2, retained). 434 - "Someone else" co-parent: person-picker dropdown (B12 fix from QA pass 2, retained). 435 - Race rescue in `apply_partner!`: rescue `ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique` and reload (B9 fix retained). 436 437 #### 3C. NI in location step 438 439 Single-line view change (already in Wave 1; ensure Wave 2 doesn't regress). 440 441 ### Commit 4 — Sessions + Auth controllers + login warning UX (~1.5 days) 442 443 **Files**: `app/controllers/mobile/sessions_controller.rb`, `app/controllers/mobile/auth_controller.rb`, `app/views/mobile/sessions/new.html.erb`, `app/views/mobile/auth/secure_account.html.erb`, `app/javascript/controllers/login_warning_controller.js`. 444 445 #### 4A. SessionsController 446 447 ```ruby 448 class Mobile::SessionsController < Mobile::BaseController 449 rate_limit to: 10, within: 3.minutes, only: :create 450 451 def new 452 # Q8: server-side check as backup for Stimulus warning 453 @has_onboarding_data = current_user&.anonymous? && current_user.current_step.present? 454 end 455 456 def create 457 user = User.authenticate_by( 458 email_address: params.require(:email_address), 459 password: params.require(:password) 460 ) 461 462 if user 463 destroy_anonymous_user! 464 session[:user_id] = user.id 465 cookies.delete :user_token 466 redirect_to mobile_dashboard_path 467 else 468 redirect_to mobile_login_path, alert: "Invalid email or password" 469 end 470 end 471 472 def destroy 473 session[:user_id] = nil 474 redirect_to mobile_intro_path 475 end 476 477 private 478 479 def destroy_anonymous_user! 480 return unless current_user&.anonymous? 481 current_user.destroy! 482 @current_user = nil 483 cookies.delete :user_token 484 end 485 end 486 ``` 487 488 #### 4B. AuthController 489 490 ```ruby 491 class Mobile::AuthController < Mobile::BaseController 492 before_action :requires_user! 493 494 def secure_account 495 return redirect_to mobile_dashboard_path if current_user.registered? 496 @user = current_user 497 end 498 499 def create 500 return redirect_to mobile_dashboard_path if current_user.registered? 501 502 user = current_user 503 user.email_address = params.require(:email_address) 504 user.password = params.require(:password) 505 506 if user.save(context: :register) 507 session[:user_id] = user.id 508 cookies.delete :user_token 509 redirect_to mobile_dashboard_path 510 else 511 @user = user 512 render :secure_account, status: :unprocessable_entity 513 end 514 end 515 end 516 ``` 517 518 #### 4C. Login warning UX (Q8 server-side backup) 519 520 `app/views/mobile/sessions/new.html.erb`: 521 522 ```erb 523 <% if @has_onboarding_data %> 524
525

Logging in will discard the information you've entered during onboarding.

526 <%= link_to "Continue with login", "#login-form", class: "btn-primary" %> 527 <%= link_to "Go back to onboarding", current_user.first_incomplete_path, class: "btn-secondary" %> 528
529 <% end %> 530 531 <%= form_with url: mobile_login_path, id: "login-form" do |f| %> 532 533 <% end %> 534 ``` 535 536 The Stimulus warning still fires on the link click (W2.9 from v3); the server-side banner provides defence-in-depth. 537 538 ### Commit 5 — Tests + verification (~1.5 days) 539 540 **Files**: spec/* additions, polish. 541 542 #### 5A. New tests required 543 544 ```ruby 545 # spec/models/user_spec.rb 546 RSpec.describe User do 547 describe ".authenticate_by" do 548 it "returns nil for anonymous Users" do 549 User.create! 550 expect(User.authenticate_by(email_address: nil, password: "x")).to be_nil 551 expect(User.authenticate_by(email_address: "", password: "x")).to be_nil 552 end 553 554 it "returns nil for Users with NULL password_digest" do 555 User.create!(email_address: "test@example.com", password_digest: nil) 556 expect(User.authenticate_by(email_address: "test@example.com", password: "x")).to be_nil 557 end 558 559 it "authenticates registered Users" do 560 User.create!(email_address: "test@example.com", password: "securepass1234") 561 expect(User.authenticate_by(email_address: "test@example.com", password: "securepass1234")).to be_present 562 end 563 564 it "is case-insensitive on email" do 565 User.create!(email_address: "Test@Example.com", password: "securepass1234") 566 expect(User.authenticate_by(email_address: "test@example.com", password: "securepass1234")).to be_present 567 end 568 end 569 570 describe "uniqueness with deterministic encryption" do 571 it "rejects duplicate emails differing only in case" do 572 User.create!(email_address: "test@example.com", password: "securepass1234") 573 duplicate = User.new(email_address: "TEST@EXAMPLE.COM", password: "securepass1234") 574 expect { duplicate.save!(context: :register) }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid, /already been taken/i) 575 end 576 end 577 578 describe "anonymous Users" do 579 it "can be created without email or password" do 580 user = User.create! 581 expect(user.anonymous?).to be true 582 expect(user.token).to be_present 583 expect(user.token_digest).to be_present 584 expect(user.draft_will).to be_present 585 end 586 587 it "has token_digest stored, not plaintext token" do 588 user = User.create! 589 raw = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.exec_query("SELECT token_digest FROM users WHERE id = #{user.id}").first 590 expect(raw["token_digest"]).not_to eq user.token # plaintext token isn't in DB 591 expect(raw["token_digest"]).to eq Digest::SHA256.hexdigest(user.token) 592 end 593 end 594 end 595 ``` 596 597 ```ruby 598 # spec/requests/mobile/onboarding_lifecycle_spec.rb 599 RSpec.describe "Onboarding cookie lifecycle" do 600 it "creates anonymous User on first onboarding action, not on marketing-page visit" do 601 expect(User.count).to eq 0 602 603 # Marketing page — does NOT create User 604 get mobile_intro_path 605 expect(User.count).to eq 0 606 expect(cookies[:user_token]).to be_blank 607 608 # First onboarding action — creates User 609 post mobile_intro_continue_path 610 expect(User.count).to eq 1 611 expect(cookies[:user_token]).to be_present 612 expect(User.last.anonymous?).to be true 613 expect(User.last.draft_will).to be_present 614 end 615 616 it "swaps user_token cookie for session_id at registration (Q12)" do 617 # Get to anonymous-User state 618 post mobile_intro_continue_path 619 expect(cookies[:user_token]).to be_present 620 user_id = User.last.id 621 622 # Register 623 post mobile_auth_secure_account_path, 624 params: { email_address: "test@example.com", password: "securepass1234" } 625 626 expect(cookies[:user_token]).to be_blank 627 expect(session[:user_id]).to eq user_id 628 end 629 end 630 ``` 631 632 #### 5B. Tests already in v3, retained 633 634 - Capybara happy-path test (Wave 1 + Wave 2 verification) 635 - Family-step "someone else" co-parent flow 636 - Relationship-status-change cascade 637 - Cohabitation `started_at` validation 638 - Step-child Parentage shape (T3) 639 - B9 race rescue test 640 641 #### 5C. Bonus polish 642 643 - `Mobile::BaseController` flow comment block at top of file (E16-style) 644 - Quick smoke test that all migrations are reversible (where reasonable) 645 646 --- 647 648 ## Done-when (Wave 2) 649 650 1. ✓ Wave 1 still passes 651 2. ✓ All onboarding steps walk happily, creating Person + Parentage + Marriage rows + an empty draft Will against the User 652 3. ✓ User row created only on meaningful action (Q2): marketing pages don't create Users 653 4. ✓ Cookies use plaintext token; DB stores token_digest (Q1) 654 5. ✓ Email normalisation works correctly with deterministic encryption (Q3); duplicate-email-with-different-case rejected 655 6. ✓ Numericality validation prevents negative `times_divorced` / `times_widowed` (Q4) 656 7. ✓ Encryption test verifies `Person.first_name` is ciphertext at rest (Q11) 657 8. ✓ Cookie cutover test verifies registration swaps user_token → session_id (Q12) 658 9. ✓ Login warning UX has both Stimulus + server-side backup (Q8) 659 10. ✓ All 8 schema migrations applied; `onboarding_sessions` table dropped; new tables exist 660 11. ✓ Anonymous Users with `last_seen_at < 3.hours.ago` are cleaned up by hourly cron 661 12. ✓ Login destroys anonymous User cascade; switches cookies; redirects to dashboard 662 13. ✓ Registration upgrades anonymous User in place; switches cookies; redirects to dashboard 663 14. ✓ Family-step UX expansion: per-child co-parent question + person-picker UX + button-disable + race rescue 664 15. ✓ All tests pass: Wave 1 + the new and retained Wave 2 tests 665 666 ## What this plan deliberately doesn't do 667 668 - No `Bequest`, `BequestAllocation`, `WillResidueAllocation`, `WillExecutor`, `WillWitness`, `Guardianship`, `Invitation`, `BeneficiaryGroup` tables. Epic 4+. 669 - No paper_trail / paranoia / postgres CHECK enums / cascade-delete test / re-encryption migration / encryption key rotation runbook / cleanup-job load test. PRE_LAUNCH_TODO. 670 - No Trust, Business, Asset. Epic 5+. 671 - No filtering of person-picker dropdown by relationship_kind (Q10 deferred to Epic 4). 672 673 ## Estimated effort 674 675 | Commit | Effort | 676 |---|---| 677 | 1 — Schema + encryption | ~3 days | 678 | 2 — User model + BaseController + cleanup job | ~2.5 days | 679 | 3 — Onboarding controller + family-step UX | ~3 days | 680 | 4 — Sessions + Auth controllers + login warning | ~1.5 days | 681 | 5 — Tests + verification | ~1.5 days | 682 | **Total Wave 2** | **~10 days** | 683 684 Wave 1: 1 day. 685 686 **Total Epic 2**: ~11 days. 687 688 Plus Epic 3 polish (register UX, login UX, session refresh, error instrumentation, push-dedup switch): ~3–4 days. 689 690 **Grand total Epic 2 + Epic 3**: ~14–15 days. 691

# Step 7 — DHH v4 final (2026-04-27) > **Supersedes v3** ([08-dhh-v3-final-merged-2026-04-27.md](08-dhh-v3-final-merged-2026-04-27.md)). Synthesised from QA pass 3 + Elon first-principles pass 3. > > v4 is the executable plan. Same overall scope as v3 (Wave 1 + unified Wave 2). Adds: token-digest pattern for cookie security, deferred User creation on marketing pages, email normalisation, validation tightening, encryption-at-rest test, and 5-commit packaging for Wave 2. ## Changes from v3 **Adopted (folded in)**: - Q1: `User.token` → `User.token_digest` for cookie security. Cookie carries plaintext, DB stores hash. - Q2: `Mobile::BaseController#ensure_user!` removed; `requires_user!` filter only on actions that need it (onboarding, auth). Marketing pages don't create User rows. - Q3: `User#normalise_email_address` callback (downcase + strip before save) — works correctly with deterministic encryption. - Q4: `numericality` validation on `times_divorced` / `times_widowed` (≥ 0). - Q6: Migration ordering note rewritten as "atomic deploy." - Q7: `User#first_incomplete_path` and `welcome_complete?`/`location_complete?`/etc. inlined explicitly. - Q8: Server-side login confirmation as backup to Stimulus warning. - Q11: Encryption-at-rest test. - Q12: Cookie cutover test at registration. - Elon E5: Wave 2 packaged as 5 atomic commits, not 12. **Adjusted with Q2 in mind**: - Will-from-day-one preserved. With Q2 in place ("only create User on meaningful action"), Wills are created for engaged users only — not for marketing-page visitors. Acceptable. **Explicitly rejected**: - Elon E2 ("don't materialize anything until welcome step"): too disruptive vs the Q2 partial fix. Q2 captures most of the benefit (no User on marketing pages); going further requires rebuilding the attribution capture mechanism. Defer to a future plan if bot traffic actually becomes a concern. - Elon E3 (drop Will-from-day-one): the user explicitly confirmed Will-from-day-one. With Q2, the cost is negligible. - Q10 (filter person-picker dropdown): keeping the long list. Epic 4 person-merge feature handles cleanup. - Q13 (cleanup job load test): deferred to PRE_LAUNCH_TODO. - Q14, Q15, Q16, Q17 nits: deferred. ## Note to AI agent Forbidden: - No service objects (`app/services/` is off limits). - No form objects, presenter objects, "policy" objects. - No new gems beyond what's already in the Gemfile. - No premature extraction of helpers, concerns, or shared modules until at least three duplicates exist. - No defensive code for cases that can't happen. - No rescuing exceptions you don't have a specific recovery for. - Multi-row write logic lives on the relevant Active Record model as fat-model methods, not in service-object classes. - No default scopes (DHH dislikes them; explicit > implicit). --- ## Wave structure (unchanged) - **Wave 1**: 1 day. Internal-testing unblock. **Identical to v3 / v2.** See [v2 §Wave 1](06-dhh-v2-final-2026-04-27.md#wave-1) for full detail. - **Wave 2**: ~10 days. Five atomic commits, ships in a single deploy. --- ## Wave 2 — commit packaging 5 commits, ordered. Each is a logically coherent atomic unit. CI runs each commit + the chain. ### Commit 1 — Schema + encryption (~3 days) **Files**: 8 migrations, encryption setup. No code change visible to users. #### 1A. Migrations Eight migrations, run in numbered order on a fresh DB. **Single deploy: all migrations + code refactor in subsequent commits land together.** ```ruby # db/migrate/20260427120001_make_user_auth_fields_nullable.rb class MakeUserAuthFieldsNullable < ActiveRecord::Migration[8.1] def change change_column_null :users, :email_address, true change_column_null :users, :password_digest, true remove_index :users, :email_address add_index :users, :email_address, unique: true, where: "email_address IS NOT NULL", name: "index_users_on_email_address_when_set" end end ``` ```ruby # db/migrate/20260427120002_add_anonymous_lifecycle_to_users.rb class AddAnonymousLifecycleToUsers < ActiveRecord::Migration[8.1] def change # Q1 fix: token_digest, NOT plaintext token add_column :users, :token_digest, :string add_index :users, :token_digest, unique: true add_column :users, :current_step, :string add_column :users, :intro_seen_at, :datetime add_column :users, :last_seen_at, :datetime add_column :users, :completed_at, :datetime add_column :users, :attribution_source, :string add_column :users, :campaign, :string add_column :users, :raw_url, :text add_column :users, :first_show, :string add_column :users, :video_intro_version, :integer add_column :users, :risk_questionnaire_version, :integer add_column :users, :video_completed_at, :datetime add_column :users, :questionnaire_completed_at, :datetime end end ``` Migrations 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 are **identical to v3** (`CreatePeople`, `AddWillMakerPersonIdToUsers`, `CreateParentages`, `CreateMarriages`, `CreateWills`, `DropOnboardingSessionsTable`). See [v3 W2.1](08-dhh-v3-final-merged-2026-04-27.md#w21--schema-migrations) for those exact migrations. #### 1B. Encryption setup ```bash bin/rails db:encryption:init ``` Add the generated keys to `config/credentials/{development,test,production}.yml.enc`. #### 1C. Tests landing in Commit 1 ```ruby # spec/models/encryption_at_rest_spec.rb (Q11) RSpec.describe "PII encryption at rest" do it "stores Person.first_name as ciphertext, never plaintext" do user = User.create! person = user.people.create!(relationship_kind: "self", first_name: "Alice") raw = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.exec_query( "SELECT first_name FROM people WHERE id = #{person.id}" ).first["first_name"] expect(raw).not_to eq "Alice" expect(raw.length).to be > 30 expect(person.first_name).to eq "Alice" end it "stores User.email_address as ciphertext" do user = User.create!(email_address: "test@example.com", password: "securepass1234") raw = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.exec_query( "SELECT email_address FROM users WHERE id = #{user.id}" ).first["email_address"] expect(raw).not_to eq "test@example.com" end end ``` ### Commit 2 — User model + BaseController + cleanup job (~2.5 days) **Files**: `app/models/user.rb`, `app/controllers/mobile/base_controller.rb`, `app/jobs/abandoned_anonymous_user_cleanup_job.rb`, schedule update for solid_queue. #### 2A. User model ```ruby class User < ApplicationRecord has_secure_password validations: false has_many :sessions, dependent: :destroy has_many :api_sessions, dependent: :destroy has_many :devices, dependent: :destroy has_many :people, dependent: :destroy belongs_to :will_maker_person, class_name: "Person", optional: true has_one :spouse_person, -> { where(relationship_kind: "spouse") }, class_name: "Person", inverse_of: :user has_many :wills, dependent: :destroy has_one :active_will, -> { where(status: "active") }, class_name: "Will" has_one :draft_will, -> { where(status: "draft") }, class_name: "Will" encrypts :email_address, deterministic: true validates :email_address, uniqueness: { case_sensitive: false }, presence: true, on: :register validates :password, length: { minimum: 12 }, format: { with: /\A(?=.*[a-zA-Z])(?=.*[0-9]).*\z/, message: "must contain letters and numbers" }, on: :register # Q3: normalise email before encryption to match deterministic encryption uniqueness before_save :normalise_email_address # Will-from-day-one after_create :create_initial_will attr_accessor :token # Q1: transient — DB stores token_digest before_create :assign_token # === State predicates === def anonymous? email_address.nil? end def registered? email_address.present? end # === Auth === def self.authenticate_by(email_address:, password:) return nil if email_address.blank? || password.blank? normalised = email_address.downcase.strip user = where.not(email_address: nil).find_by(email_address: normalised) return nil if user.nil? || user.password_digest.blank? user.authenticate(password) ? user : nil end def self.find_by_cookie_token(plaintext) return nil if plaintext.blank? digest = Digest::SHA256.hexdigest(plaintext) find_by(token_digest: digest, email_address: nil) end # === Onboarding step predicates (Q7 inlined) === def first_incomplete_path return Rails.application.routes.url_helpers.mobile_onboarding_welcome_path unless welcome_complete? return Rails.application.routes.url_helpers.mobile_onboarding_location_path unless location_complete? return Rails.application.routes.url_helpers.mobile_onboarding_family_path unless family_complete? return Rails.application.routes.url_helpers.mobile_onboarding_extended_family_path unless extended_family_complete? Rails.application.routes.url_helpers.mobile_onboarding_wrap_up_path end def welcome_complete? will_maker_person.present? && will_maker_person.first_name.present? && will_maker_person.last_name.present? && will_maker_person.date_of_birth.present? end def location_complete? will_maker_person.present? && will_maker_person.country_of_residence.present? && will_maker_person.nationality.present? && will_maker_person.domiciled_in_uk.present? && will_maker_person.currently_resident_in_uk.present? end def family_complete? current_step.in?(%w[extended_family wrap_up]) || completed_at.present? end def extended_family_complete? current_step == "wrap_up" || completed_at.present? end # === Lifecycle === def inactive_for_more_than?(duration) last_seen_at && last_seen_at < duration.ago end def touch_last_seen! update_column(:last_seen_at, Time.current) end # === Onboarding step writes (fat-model methods) === # apply_welcome_step!, apply_location_step!, apply_family_step!, # apply_extended_family_step!, apply_partner!, apply_children!, apply_co_parent!, # destroy_existing_partnership!, destroy_orphaned_co_parents! # Implementation identical to v2 [§W2.5]; just operates on `self.people` not `onboarding_session.people`. private def normalise_email_address self.email_address = email_address.downcase.strip if email_address.present? end def assign_token self.token = SecureRandom.hex(32) self.token_digest = Digest::SHA256.hexdigest(token) end def create_initial_will wills.create!(version: 1, status: "draft") end end ``` Plus `Person` model adds `numericality` validation: ```ruby validates :times_divorced, :times_widowed, numericality: { greater_than_or_equal_to: 0, only_integer: true } ``` #### 2B. BaseController (Q2 fix folded in) ```ruby class Mobile::BaseController < ApplicationController helper_method :current_user before_action :touch_user_if_present private def current_user @current_user ||= find_current_user end def find_current_user if user_id = session[:user_id] registered = User.find_by(id: user_id) return registered if registered session.delete(:user_id) # stale; clear end if cookie_token = cookies.signed[:user_token] anonymous = User.find_by_cookie_token(cookie_token) return anonymous if anonymous && !stale?(anonymous) cleanup_stale_user!(anonymous, cookie_token) if anonymous end nil end # Only call from controllers that need a User (onboarding, auth). # Marketing pages (intro, video-intro, risk-questionnaire) do NOT create Users. def requires_user! @current_user = current_user || create_anonymous_user! end def create_anonymous_user! user = User.create! cookies.signed[:user_token] = { value: user.token, # transient plaintext expires: 3.hours.from_now, httponly: true, same_site: :lax, secure: Rails.env.production? } user end def stale?(user) user.last_seen_at.present? && user.last_seen_at < 3.hours.ago end def cleanup_stale_user!(stale_user, cookie_token) stale_user.destroy cookies.delete :user_token @current_user = nil end def touch_user_if_present current_user&.touch_last_seen! if current_user&.anonymous? end def authenticate_user! return if current_user&.registered? redirect_to mobile_login_path end end ``` #### 2C. Controllers that require_user! ```ruby class Mobile::OnboardingController < Mobile::BaseController before_action :requires_user! # ... (W2.5 logic, calling current_user.apply_*_step!) end class Mobile::AuthController < Mobile::BaseController before_action :requires_user! # ... (W2.7 logic) end ``` `Mobile::StartupController` (which renders /mobile, /mobile/intro, /mobile/video-intro, /mobile/risk-questionnaire) does **NOT** call `requires_user!`. These are visible to bots without creating User rows. When the user clicks "Continue" on the intro step (which POSTs to `/mobile/intro/continue`), the receiving action calls `requires_user!` — User created at that moment, attribution captured, redirect to first onboarding step. #### 2D. Cleanup job ```ruby class AbandonedAnonymousUserCleanupJob < ApplicationJob queue_as :default def perform User.where(email_address: nil) .where("last_seen_at < ? OR (last_seen_at IS NULL AND created_at < ?)", 3.hours.ago, 3.hours.ago) .find_each(batch_size: 100, &:destroy) end end ``` Schedule via Solid Queue cron (or whatever scheduler is in use) — runs hourly. ### Commit 3 — Onboarding controller + family-step UX (~3 days) **Files**: `app/controllers/mobile/onboarding_controller.rb`, `app/views/mobile/onboarding/*`, `app/javascript/controllers/family_form_controller.js`. #### 3A. OnboardingController refactor Replace every `onboarding_session` reference with `current_user`. Per-action logic: ```ruby class Mobile::OnboardingController < Mobile::BaseController before_action :requires_user! def update_welcome current_user.apply_welcome_step!(welcome_params) redirect_to mobile_onboarding_location_path rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e @errors = e.record.errors render :welcome, status: :unprocessable_entity end # update_location, update_family, update_extended_family, etc. follow the same pattern. private def welcome_params params.require(:person).permit(:first_name, :middle_names, :last_name, :date_of_birth) end def family_params params.permit( :relationship_kind, :marriage_kind, :times_divorced, :times_widowed, :has_children, partner: [:first_name, :last_name, :started_at], children: [ :first_name, :last_name, :date_of_birth, :relationship_kind, :disabled, :lacks_mental_capacity, co_parent: [:type, :existing_person_id, :first_name, :last_name, :relationship_to_child] ] ) end end ``` #### 3B. Family-step UX expansion Per-child co-parent question — see [v3 W2.8](08-dhh-v3-final-merged-2026-04-27.md#w28--family-step-ux-expansion) for full template + Stimulus controller. Changes from v3 plan: - Submit button: `data: { disable_with: "Saving..." }` (B9 fix from QA pass 2, retained). - "Someone else" co-parent: person-picker dropdown (B12 fix from QA pass 2, retained). - Race rescue in `apply_partner!`: rescue `ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique` and reload (B9 fix retained). #### 3C. NI in location step Single-line view change (already in Wave 1; ensure Wave 2 doesn't regress). ### Commit 4 — Sessions + Auth controllers + login warning UX (~1.5 days) **Files**: `app/controllers/mobile/sessions_controller.rb`, `app/controllers/mobile/auth_controller.rb`, `app/views/mobile/sessions/new.html.erb`, `app/views/mobile/auth/secure_account.html.erb`, `app/javascript/controllers/login_warning_controller.js`. #### 4A. SessionsController ```ruby class Mobile::SessionsController < Mobile::BaseController rate_limit to: 10, within: 3.minutes, only: :create def new # Q8: server-side check as backup for Stimulus warning @has_onboarding_data = current_user&.anonymous? && current_user.current_step.present? end def create user = User.authenticate_by( email_address: params.require(:email_address), password: params.require(:password) ) if user destroy_anonymous_user! session[:user_id] = user.id cookies.delete :user_token redirect_to mobile_dashboard_path else redirect_to mobile_login_path, alert: "Invalid email or password" end end def destroy session[:user_id] = nil redirect_to mobile_intro_path end private def destroy_anonymous_user! return unless current_user&.anonymous? current_user.destroy! @current_user = nil cookies.delete :user_token end end ``` #### 4B. AuthController ```ruby class Mobile::AuthController < Mobile::BaseController before_action :requires_user! def secure_account return redirect_to mobile_dashboard_path if current_user.registered? @user = current_user end def create return redirect_to mobile_dashboard_path if current_user.registered? user = current_user user.email_address = params.require(:email_address) user.password = params.require(:password) if user.save(context: :register) session[:user_id] = user.id cookies.delete :user_token redirect_to mobile_dashboard_path else @user = user render :secure_account, status: :unprocessable_entity end end end ``` #### 4C. Login warning UX (Q8 server-side backup) `app/views/mobile/sessions/new.html.erb`: ```erb <% if @has_onboarding_data %>

Logging in will discard the information you've entered during onboarding.

<%= link_to "Continue with login", "#login-form", class: "btn-primary" %> <%= link_to "Go back to onboarding", current_user.first_incomplete_path, class: "btn-secondary" %>
<% end %> <%= form_with url: mobile_login_path, id: "login-form" do |f| %> <% end %> ``` The Stimulus warning still fires on the link click (W2.9 from v3); the server-side banner provides defence-in-depth. ### Commit 5 — Tests + verification (~1.5 days) **Files**: spec/* additions, polish. #### 5A. New tests required ```ruby # spec/models/user_spec.rb RSpec.describe User do describe ".authenticate_by" do it "returns nil for anonymous Users" do User.create! expect(User.authenticate_by(email_address: nil, password: "x")).to be_nil expect(User.authenticate_by(email_address: "", password: "x")).to be_nil end it "returns nil for Users with NULL password_digest" do User.create!(email_address: "test@example.com", password_digest: nil) expect(User.authenticate_by(email_address: "test@example.com", password: "x")).to be_nil end it "authenticates registered Users" do User.create!(email_address: "test@example.com", password: "securepass1234") expect(User.authenticate_by(email_address: "test@example.com", password: "securepass1234")).to be_present end it "is case-insensitive on email" do User.create!(email_address: "Test@Example.com", password: "securepass1234") expect(User.authenticate_by(email_address: "test@example.com", password: "securepass1234")).to be_present end end describe "uniqueness with deterministic encryption" do it "rejects duplicate emails differing only in case" do User.create!(email_address: "test@example.com", password: "securepass1234") duplicate = User.new(email_address: "TEST@EXAMPLE.COM", password: "securepass1234") expect { duplicate.save!(context: :register) }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid, /already been taken/i) end end describe "anonymous Users" do it "can be created without email or password" do user = User.create! expect(user.anonymous?).to be true expect(user.token).to be_present expect(user.token_digest).to be_present expect(user.draft_will).to be_present end it "has token_digest stored, not plaintext token" do user = User.create! raw = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.exec_query("SELECT token_digest FROM users WHERE id = #{user.id}").first expect(raw["token_digest"]).not_to eq user.token # plaintext token isn't in DB expect(raw["token_digest"]).to eq Digest::SHA256.hexdigest(user.token) end end end ``` ```ruby # spec/requests/mobile/onboarding_lifecycle_spec.rb RSpec.describe "Onboarding cookie lifecycle" do it "creates anonymous User on first onboarding action, not on marketing-page visit" do expect(User.count).to eq 0 # Marketing page — does NOT create User get mobile_intro_path expect(User.count).to eq 0 expect(cookies[:user_token]).to be_blank # First onboarding action — creates User post mobile_intro_continue_path expect(User.count).to eq 1 expect(cookies[:user_token]).to be_present expect(User.last.anonymous?).to be true expect(User.last.draft_will).to be_present end it "swaps user_token cookie for session_id at registration (Q12)" do # Get to anonymous-User state post mobile_intro_continue_path expect(cookies[:user_token]).to be_present user_id = User.last.id # Register post mobile_auth_secure_account_path, params: { email_address: "test@example.com", password: "securepass1234" } expect(cookies[:user_token]).to be_blank expect(session[:user_id]).to eq user_id end end ``` #### 5B. Tests already in v3, retained - Capybara happy-path test (Wave 1 + Wave 2 verification) - Family-step "someone else" co-parent flow - Relationship-status-change cascade - Cohabitation `started_at` validation - Step-child Parentage shape (T3) - B9 race rescue test #### 5C. Bonus polish - `Mobile::BaseController` flow comment block at top of file (E16-style) - Quick smoke test that all migrations are reversible (where reasonable) --- ## Done-when (Wave 2) 1. ✓ Wave 1 still passes 2. ✓ All onboarding steps walk happily, creating Person + Parentage + Marriage rows + an empty draft Will against the User 3. ✓ User row created only on meaningful action (Q2): marketing pages don't create Users 4. ✓ Cookies use plaintext token; DB stores token_digest (Q1) 5. ✓ Email normalisation works correctly with deterministic encryption (Q3); duplicate-email-with-different-case rejected 6. ✓ Numericality validation prevents negative `times_divorced` / `times_widowed` (Q4) 7. ✓ Encryption test verifies `Person.first_name` is ciphertext at rest (Q11) 8. ✓ Cookie cutover test verifies registration swaps user_token → session_id (Q12) 9. ✓ Login warning UX has both Stimulus + server-side backup (Q8) 10. ✓ All 8 schema migrations applied; `onboarding_sessions` table dropped; new tables exist 11. ✓ Anonymous Users with `last_seen_at < 3.hours.ago` are cleaned up by hourly cron 12. ✓ Login destroys anonymous User cascade; switches cookies; redirects to dashboard 13. ✓ Registration upgrades anonymous User in place; switches cookies; redirects to dashboard 14. ✓ Family-step UX expansion: per-child co-parent question + person-picker UX + button-disable + race rescue 15. ✓ All tests pass: Wave 1 + the new and retained Wave 2 tests ## What this plan deliberately doesn't do - No `Bequest`, `BequestAllocation`, `WillResidueAllocation`, `WillExecutor`, `WillWitness`, `Guardianship`, `Invitation`, `BeneficiaryGroup` tables. Epic 4+. - No paper_trail / paranoia / postgres CHECK enums / cascade-delete test / re-encryption migration / encryption key rotation runbook / cleanup-job load test. PRE_LAUNCH_TODO. - No Trust, Business, Asset. Epic 5+. - No filtering of person-picker dropdown by relationship_kind (Q10 deferred to Epic 4). ## Estimated effort | Commit | Effort | |---|---| | 1 — Schema + encryption | ~3 days | | 2 — User model + BaseController + cleanup job | ~2.5 days | | 3 — Onboarding controller + family-step UX | ~3 days | | 4 — Sessions + Auth controllers + login warning | ~1.5 days | | 5 — Tests + verification | ~1.5 days | | **Total Wave 2** | **~10 days** | Wave 1: 1 day. **Total Epic 2**: ~11 days. Plus Epic 3 polish (register UX, login UX, session refresh, error instrumentation, push-dedup switch): ~3–4 days. **Grand total Epic 2 + Epic 3**: ~14–15 days.

  # These are extensions that must be enabled in order to support this database
  enable_extension "pg_catalog.plpgsql"

  create_table "api_sessions", force: :cascade do |t|
    t.datetime "access_expires_at", null: false
    t.string "access_token_digest", null: false
    t.datetime "created_at", null: false
    t.string "device_id"
    t.string "device_name"
    t.datetime "refresh_expires_at", null: false
    t.string "refresh_token_digest", null: false
    t.datetime "revoked_at"

    t.index ["access_token_digest"], name: "index_api_sessions_on_access_token_digest", unique: true
    t.index ["refresh_expires_at"], name: "index_api_sessions_on_refresh_expires_at"
    t.index ["refresh_token_digest"], name: "index_api_sessions_on_refresh_token_digest", unique: true
    t.index ["user_id"], name: "index_api_sessions_on_user_id"
  end

  create_table "devices", force: :cascade do |t|
    t.string "apns_token", null: false
    t.datetime "created_at", null: false
    t.datetime "last_registered_at", null: false
    t.bigint "onboarding_session_id"
    t.string "platform", null: false
    t.datetime "updated_at", null: false
    t.bigint "user_id"
    t.string "vendor_id"
    t.index ["apns_token"], name: "index_devices_on_apns_token", unique: true
    t.index ["onboarding_session_id"], name: "index_devices_on_onboarding_session_id"
    t.index ["user_id"], name: "index_devices_on_user_id"
    t.index ["vendor_id"], name: "index_devices_on_vendor_id", unique: true
  end

  create_table "motor_alert_locks", force: :cascade do |t|
    t.bigint "alert_id", null: false
    t.datetime "created_at", null: false
    t.string "lock_timestamp", null: false
    t.datetime "updated_at", null: false
    t.index ["alert_id", "lock_timestamp"], name: "index_motor_alert_locks_on_alert_id_and_lock_timestamp", unique: true
    t.index ["alert_id"], name: "index_motor_alert_locks_on_alert_id"
  end

  create_table "motor_alerts", force: :cascade do |t|
    t.bigint "author_id"
    t.string "author_type"
    t.datetime "created_at", null: false
    t.datetime "deleted_at"
    t.text "description"
    t.boolean "is_enabled", default: true, null: false
    t.string "name", null: false
    t.text "preferences", null: false
    t.bigint "query_id", null: false
    t.text "to_emails", null: false
    t.datetime "updated_at", null: false
    t.index ["name"], name: "motor_alerts_name_unique_index", unique: true, where: "(deleted_at IS NULL)"
    t.index ["query_id"], name: "index_motor_alerts_on_query_id"
    t.index ["updated_at"], name: "index_motor_alerts_on_updated_at"
  end

  create_table "motor_api_configs", force: :cascade do |t|
    t.datetime "created_at", null: false
    t.text "credentials", null: false
    t.datetime "deleted_at"
    t.text "description"
    t.string "name", null: false

file.content:
class User < ApplicationRecord
  has_secure_password
  has_many :sessions, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :api_sessions, dependent: :destroy

  normalizes :email_address, with: ->(e) { e.strip.downcase }
  
  validates :first_name, presence: true
  validates :last_name, presence: true
  validates :email_address,
            presence: true,
            uniqueness: true,
            format: { with: /\A[^@\s]+@[^@\s]+\.[^@\s]+\z/ }
  validate :password_complexity, if: -> { password.present? }

  enum :status, { active: "active", locked: "locked", suspended: "suspended" }, default: "active"
  
  def full_name
    "#{first_name} #{last_name}".strip
  end

  private

  def password_complexity
    unless password.length >= 12 && password.match?(/[A-Za-z]/) && password.match?(/\d/)
      errors.add(:password, "must be at least 12 characters and include letters and numbers")
    end
  end
end

```ruby
    add_index :people, :user_id,
```

### W2.2 — Active Record encryption setup

```bash
bin/rails db:encryption:init
```

Add the generated keys to `config/credentials/{development,test,production}.yml.enc`.

```ruby
# app/models/person.rb

         :address_line_1, :address_line_2, :city, :postcode,
         deterministic: true
```

    remove_index :users, :email_address
    add_index :users, :email_address,
      unique: true,
      where: "email_address IS NOT NULL",
      name: "index_users_on_email_address_when_set"
  end
end

# Wave 2 Commit 1, migration 3/8.
#
# `people` is the master table for everyone the will-maker mentions:
# themselves (relationship_kind = "self"), their spouse/partner, children,
# co-parents, etc. Per-User uniqueness on the will-maker and spouse rows
# is enforced via partial indexes.
#
# PII columns (first_name, addresses, etc.) are encrypted in the model layer
# via `encrypts ..., deterministic: true` (added in Commit 2). Deterministic
# encryption is required so we can still query/index/uniq those columns.
class CreatePeople < ActiveRecord::Migration[8.1]
  def change
    create_table :people do |t|
      t.references :user, foreign_key: true, null: false
      t.string :relationship_kind, null: false, default: "other"
      t.integer :position, null: false, default: 0
      t.string :first_name
      t.string :middle_names
      t.string :last_name
      t.date :date_of_birth
      t.string :email
      t.string :phone
      t.string :address_line_1
      t.string :address_line_2
      t.string :city
      t.string :postcode
      t.string :country_of_residence
      t.string :nationality
      t.string :domiciled_in_uk
      t.string :currently_resident_in_uk
      t.boolean :disabled, null: false, default: false
      t.boolean :lacks_mental_capacity, null: false, default: false
      t.integer :times_divorced, null: false, default: 0
      t.integer :times_widowed, null: false, default: 0
      t.string :parents_alive
      t.string :parents_in_law_alive
      t.string :siblings_alive
      t.integer :number_of_siblings
      t.timestamps
    end

    # One will-maker Person per User; one spouse Person per User.
    add_index :people, :user_id,
      unique: true,
      where: "relationship_kind = 'self'",
      name: "index_people_on_user_will_maker"

    add_index :people, :user_id,
      unique: true,
      where: "relationship_kind = 'spouse'",
      name: "index_people_on_user_spouse"
  end
end

# Wave 2 Commit 1, migration 5/8.
#
# Typed parent-child relationships. `kind` distinguishes biological from
# adoptive/step/foster, which matters for inheritance law (e.g. step
# children aren't automatic legal heirs in England & Wales). The same
# child Person can have multiple Parentage rows for different parents
# of different kinds.
class CreateParentages < ActiveRecord::Migration[8.1]
  def change
    create_table :parentages do |t|
      t.references :parent_person, foreign_key: { to_table: :people }, null: false
      t.references :child_person, foreign_key: { to_table: :people }, null: false
      t.string :kind, null: false  # biological | adoptive | step | foster
      t.timestamps
    end

    add_index :parentages, [ :parent_person_id, :child_person_id ], unique: true
    add_check_constraint :parentages,
      "parent_person_id != child_person_id",
      name: "parentages_distinct_persons"
  end
end

# Wave 2 Commit 1, migration 6/8.
#
# Typed partnership row between will-maker and partner Person. `phase`
# tracks active vs ended; partial unique index on `phase = 'active'`
# enforces "one current partnership per will-maker".
#
# A user with previous marriages keeps an `ended` row per prior
# relationship — important for divorce-status / widowed history that
# affects estate planning.
class CreateMarriages < ActiveRecord::Migration[8.1]
  def change
    create_table :marriages do |t|
      t.references :will_maker_person, foreign_key: { to_table: :people }, null: false
      t.references :partner_person, foreign_key: { to_table: :people }, null: false
      t.string :kind, null: false   # married | civil_partnership | cohabiting
      t.string :phase, null: false, default: "active"
      t.date :started_at
      t.date :ended_at
      t.timestamps
    end

    add_check_constraint :marriages,
      "will_maker_person_id != partner_person_id",
      name: "marriages_distinct_persons"

    add_index :marriages,
      [ :will_maker_person_id, :partner_person_id ],
      unique: true,
      name: "index_marriages_on_pair"

    add_index :marriages,
      :will_maker_person_id,
      unique: true,
      where: "phase = 'active'",
      name: "index_marriages_on_active"
  end
end

# Wave 2 Commit 1, migration 7/8.
#
# Will-from-day-one: every User gets an empty draft Will the moment their
# row is created (anonymous or registered). `version` increments per
# subsequent revision; `supersedes_id` points at the prior version.
# Partial unique index on `status = 'active'` enforces "one active Will
# per User" without preventing draft + active to coexist during edits.
class CreateWills < ActiveRecord::Migration[8.1]
  def change
    create_table :wills do |t|
      t.references :user, foreign_key: true, null: false
      t.integer :version, null: false, default: 1
      t.string :status, null: false, default: "draft"
      t.references :supersedes, foreign_key: { to_table: :wills }, null: true
      t.datetime :finalized_at
      t.timestamps
    end

    add_index :wills, [ :user_id, :version ], unique: true
    add_index :wills, :user_id,
      unique: true,
      where: "status = 'active'",
      name: "index_wills_on_active"
  end
end

# Wave 2 Commit 1, migration 8/8.
#
# Drops the legacy `onboarding_sessions` table after the controller
# refactor (Commit 3) has migrated all reads/writes onto `current_user` +
# `users.people`. We're not live, so no data preservation.
#
# Run order discipline: this migration MUST run *after* Commit 3's code
# refactor lands, so the running app never references the dropped table.
# In a single deploy that's atomic; in dev we land migrations 1–7 first,
# refactor in Commits 2–3, then run this last.
class DropOnboardingSessionsTable < ActiveRecord::Migration[8.1]
  def up
    # Devices currently FK to onboarding_sessions; drop that reference too.
    remove_reference :devices, :onboarding_session, if_exists: true
    drop_table :onboarding_sessions, force: :cascade
  end

  def down
    raise ActiveRecord::IrreversibleMigration
  end
end

**Done**
- 7 additive migrations applied to dev + test DBs
- `users` is now anonymous-capable (nullable email/password, `token_digest`, `current_step`, attribution columns, lifecycle timestamps, `will_maker_person_id`)
- `people`, `parentages`, `marriages`, `wills` tables exist with all the indexes/checks the plan specifies
- Schema.rb regenerated; full test suite (110 unit, 3 system) still green

**Held back**
- Migration 8 (drop `onboarding_sessions`) — runs after the controller refactor in Commit 3. File is intentionally not yet on disk.
- `Person` model (with `encrypts :first_name, …`) and the User model rewrite — Commit 2.

**Blocker before Commit 2 — needs you**
Active Record encryption keys. I ran `bin/rails db:encryption:init` and got a sample block:

```yaml
active_record_encryption:
  primary_key: 6t8jD4pQWcYVRpixKxZ1DcUAHwiX1YZe
  deterministic_key: qP9bOh318R4tmB3XZrwcOZnccD8JxKkl
  key_derivation_salt: JpeIcXnseNqHjygsiYAtAG1ulyLD7Gqp
```

…but `config/master.key` isn't present on this machine, so I can't write into `credentials.yml.enc`. You'll need to:

1. Drop `config/master.key` (or set `RAILS_MASTER_KEY` env var)
2. `EDITOR="code --wait" bin/rails credentials:edit` (or your preferred editor) and paste the block above (or regenerate with `bin/rails db:encryption:init` for fresh keys — your call)
3. Same for `RAILS_ENV=test bin/rails credentials:edit --environment test` if test creds are separate

Once that's in place, ping me and I'll plough into Commit 2 (User model rewrite + Person/Marriage/Parentage models + BaseController + cleanup job).

message.content: walk me through exactly which option and how to do the desited option

content.text:
## Recommendation: per-environment credentials

Your repo currently has the older single-file pattern (`config/credentials.yml.enc` + `config/master.key`), and you don't have the master.key on this machine. Rather than hunt that down, switch dev and test to **per-environment credentials** — each environment gets its own key file. Production stays as-is (it'll get the encryption block when whoever has prod credentials adds it).

This is the modern Rails default and cleanly avoids needing the legacy master.key just to make local encryption work.

## Exact steps

### 1. Create the dev credentials file

```bash
cd /Users/lukeaikman/Documents/Sites/kindling-parent/kindling-monorepo/rails
EDITOR="code --wait" bin/rails credentials:edit --environment development
```

(Swap `code --wait` for whatever editor you use — `vim`, `nano`, `cursor --wait`, etc.)

Rails will:
- Create `config/credentials/development.key` (auto-gitignored — Rails adds `.key` files to `.gitignore` by default)
- Create `config/credentials/development.yml.enc` (encrypted, safe to commit)
- Open the decrypted file in your editor

In the open file, **paste this** (these are dev-only keys, fine to use as-is):

```yaml
active_record_encryption:
  primary_key: 6t8jD4pQWcYVRpixKxZ1DcUAHwiX1YZe
  deterministic_key: qP9bOh318R4tmB3XZrwcOZnccD8JxKkl
  key_derivation_salt: JpeIcXnseNqHjygsiYAtAG1ulyLD7Gqp
```

Save and close the editor. Rails re-encrypts the file.

### 2. Same for test

```bash
EDITOR="code --wait" bin/rails credentials:edit --environment test
```

Paste the **same block** (dev and test sharing keys is fine — neither holds real PII). Save, close.

### 3. Verify

```bash
EDITOR=cat bin/rails credentials:show --environment development
EDITOR=cat bin/rails credentials:show --environment test
```

Both should print the `active_record_encryption:` block back at you.

### 4. Confirm `.gitignore` is right

Rails should have appended these automatically — verify with:

```bash
grep credentials .gitignore
```

Expect to see something like `/config/credentials/*.key` (so `.key` files stay local, `.yml.enc` files get committed).

### 5. Production (do later — non-blocking)

Whoever has prod's `config/master.key`:

```bash
EDITOR="..." bin/rails credentials:edit
```

…and adds the same `active_record_encryption:` block — but with **fresh** keys generated via `bin/rails db:encryption:init` (don't reuse the dev keys above). Once production has its own block, anything written to those encrypted columns in prod is readable.

---

When you've done steps 1–4, say "done" and I'll start Commit 2 (User model rewrite + Person/Marriage/Parentage models + BaseController + cleanup job).

message.content:

…and adds the same active_record_encryption: block — but with fresh keys generated via bin/rails db:encryption:init (don't reuse the dev keys above). Once production has its own block, anything written to those encrypted columns in prod is readable.

1	# Pre-launch TODO
2	
3	Items deferred during phase work that MUST be addressed before the app ships to TestFlight / production. Each entry: what, why, rough effort, origin phase.
4	
5	## Cache-Control header on `Mobile::ConfigController#show`
6	
7	**What**: add `expires_in` or explicit `Cache-Control` header to the `path_configuration.json` response so CDNs and proxies can cache-and-revalidate.
8	
9	```ruby
10	def show
11	  return head :not_found unless params[:resource] == "path_configuration"
12	
13	  path = Rails.root.join("config/mobile/path_configuration.json")
14	  expires_in 5.minutes, public: true, must_revalidate: true
15	  if stale?(etag: path, last_modified: path.mtime)
16	    render json: path.read
17	  end
18	end
19	```
20	
21	**Why**: Phase C's `stale?` sets `ETag` and `Last-Modified` but no `Cache-Control`. A Rails-direct client (URLSession) respects ETag/Last-Modified anyway. A Cloudflare / Fastly / nginx layer in front of production would not know how to cache the response without `Cache-Control: public, max-age=...`. Adding it reduces origin load once real traffic hits.
22	
23	**Effort**: 1 line. Add a request spec that asserts `Cache-Control` is present.
24	
25	**Origin**: Phase C detail plan (§1.2 ConfigController). Deferred because no CDN fronts the dev environment and Phase C's scope doesn't include production infrastructure tuning.
26	
27	---
28	
29	<!-- Add new entries below as they land. Keep each self-contained: what, why, effort, origin. -->
30	
31	---
32	
33	
34	## Universal Link verification — deep-link handoff v2
35	
36	**What**: wire Apple Universal Links so `kindling.app/mobile/open?...` links tapped in Mail / Messages / Safari / ad creatives open the iOS shell directly, skipping the Safari-tap-then-"Open in Kindling"-banner dance.
37	
38	Four pieces. All four required together — partial wiring silently fails the first tap from a real device.
39	
40	1. **Rails — AASA file.** Serve `apple-app-site-association` at `https://<prod-domain>/.well-known/apple-app-site-association`. Content-Type MUST be `application/json`. The URL MUST NOT have a file extension (no `.json` in the path). Apple's CDN fetches once at install and caches up to a week. Body:
41	
42	   ```json
43	   {
44	     "applinks": {
45	       "apps": [],
46	       "details": [
47	         { "appID": "<TEAM_ID>.app.kindling.ios",
48	           "paths": ["/mobile/*"] }
49	       ]
50	     }
51	   }
52	   ```
53	
54	   Add a route: `get "/.well-known/apple-app-site-association", to: "well_known#aasa"`. Controller action renders the JSON inline (from a constant or `config/well_known/aasa.json`). Public, no CSRF, no auth.
55	
56	2. **Xcode — Associated Domains entitlement.** Open `ios/Kindling.xcodeproj`, target `Kindling`, Signing & Capabilities → `+ Capability` → Associated Domains. Add:
57	   - `applinks:kindling.app`
58	   - `applinks:staging.kindling.app` (if staging exists at v2 time)
59	
60	   This writes a new `Kindling.entitlements` file referenced by Debug + Release build configs.
61	
62	3. **AppDelegate — handler.** Add to `ios/Kindling/AppDelegate.swift`:
63	
64	   ```swift
65	   func application(
66	     _ application: UIApplication,
67	     continue userActivity: NSUserActivity,
68	     restorationHandler: @escaping ([UIUserActivityRestoring]?) -> Void
69	   ) -> Bool {
70	     guard userActivity.activityType == NSUserActivityTypeBrowsingWeb,
71	           let url = userActivity.webpageURL else { return false }
72	     Navigator.shared?.route(url)
73	     return true
74	   }
75	   ```
76	
77	   Navigator currently lives on `SceneController` as a `lazy var`. You'll need to expose it — either store it on the scene delegate and reach it from AppDelegate, or move URL handling into `SceneController`'s `scene(_:continue:)` (preferred on iOS 13+). Pick one, don't do both.
78	
79	4. **Physical-device verification.** UL signatures validate against Apple's CDN-cached AASA, which means simulator cannot test this — the check passes in simulator regardless of wiring. Must be a TestFlight build or signed ad-hoc IPA on a real device, with the real domain reachable. Sequence: cold-kill Kindling → in Notes, paste `https://kindling.app/mobile/open?source=email&campaign=welcome&show_video=1` → tap → app opens directly (no Safari flash) → lands on `/mobile/video-intro`. Warm-start from the same link works identically. Repeat with attribution already stored (`first_touch=false` in the log) — destination should still respect onboarding state, not the URL params.
80	
81	**Why**: deferred from Phase E v1 because no external link surface existed at that time. Gated on four prerequisites any one of which blocks real verification:
82	- production domain + DNS
83	- Apple Developer Team ID + App Store Connect record
84	- signed TestFlight or ad-hoc build
85	- physical iOS device in hand
86	
87	Building UL against placeholder values produces plumbing that looks fine on inspection and fails the first real-device tap — worse than having none.
88	
89	**Effort**: 1–2 days once prerequisites are met. Longer if domain/DNS/TestFlight need bootstrapping. Write as `PHASE_E2_UNIVERSAL_LINKS_DETAILED_PLAN.md` mirroring Phase C's structure when the first campaign date is set.
90	
91	**Origin**: Phase E detail plan — deferred deliberately. See `rails/planning/native-to-rails/epic-0-mobile-frontend-shell/phase-e-deeplink/PHASE_E_DEEPLINK_DETAILED_PLAN.md`.
92	
93	---
94	
95	## Postgres-level constraint hardening for string enums
96	
97	**What**: replace controller-level enum validation with Postgres native enums or `CHECK (column IN (...))` constraints for every enum-style string column.
98	
99	Affected columns (non-exhaustive, refresh from ERD before implementing):
100	- `Will.status`: `draft | active | superseded`
101	- `Marriage.kind`: `married | civil_partnership | cohabiting`
102	- `Marriage.phase`: `active | separated | divorced | widowed`
103	- `Parentage.kind`: `biological | adoptive | step | foster`
104	- `Person.relationship_kind`: `spouse | partner | child | parent | sibling | extended_family | friend | professional | other`
105	- `Invitation.role_type`: `executor | guardian`
106	- `Invitation.status`: `draft | sent | accepted | declined | expired | revoked`
107	- `Asset.held_in_trust`: `yes | no | not_sure`
108	- All `ownership_type`, `account_type`, `policy_type`, `pension_type`, `share_class`, `crypto_type`, `debt_kind`, `trust_type` columns
109	
110	Decide between Postgres native ENUM types (immutable, requires migration ceremony to add values) and CHECK constraints (mutable, simpler). Recommendation: CHECK constraints — fewer schema migrations as product evolves.
111	
112	**Why**: app-level validation can be bypassed via raw SQL, console mistakes, or migration scripts. For an estate-planning app where bad enum values cause silent will-rendering bugs ("this person's role is 'exectutor'" — typo never flagged) the DB-level guarantee matters.
113	
114	**Effort**: 2–4 hours migration writing + per-column CHECK constraint add. Plus updating model enums to match. Test by attempting to insert invalid values via raw SQL.
115	
116	**Origin**: ERD CTO review 2026-04-27. Deferred because Epic 2 v2 plan ships with controller-level validation; DB hardening is launch-readiness, not feature work.
117	
118	---
119	
120	## Active Record encryption mode audit
121	
122	**What**: review every `encrypted` column and decide deterministic vs non-deterministic mode based on actual usage.
123	
124	For v1 internal-testing, every encrypted column defaults to `deterministic: true` (allows querying / dedupe but is the weaker mode — vulnerable to frequency analysis with DB access). Before public launch, audit and flip to `deterministic: false` (stronger) for any column that doesn't need to be searched.
125	
126	Expected outcome of audit:
127	
128	| Column | Final mode | Rationale |
129	|---|---|---|
130	| `User.email_address`, `Person.email`, `Invitation.recipient_email` | deterministic | needed for unique-index lookup, dedupe, invite-match |
131	| `Person.phone`, `Invitation.recipient_phone` | non-deterministic | not searched directly |
132	| Names (`first_name`, `middle_names`, `last_name`) | non-deterministic | privacy; no unique-by-name semantics |
133	| `date_of_birth` | non-deterministic | privacy |
134	| Address fields | non-deterministic | privacy |
135	| Account / policy / sort numbers | non-deterministic | high-sensitivity PII |
136	
137	Re-encryption migration runs once: load all rows, decrypt with old config, re-encrypt with new config, save.
138	
139	**Why**: deterministic encryption is the weaker AR encryption mode. For an estate-planning app holding wills, DOBs, account numbers, and health-relevant data (disability, capacity), the threat model on a future production breach is non-trivial. Defaulting deterministic is fine for dev/internal-testing where data is fictional, but going public with deterministic-everywhere would be a privacy regression vs the stronger mode.
140	
141	**Effort**: 30 min audit + 1 hour migration + integration test verifying decrypted reads still work.
142	
143	**Origin**: ERD CTO review 2026-04-27. Deferred per "make all deterministic in dev, lock down before golive" call.
144	
145	---
146	
147	## Audit trail (paper_trail or equivalent) for legal-significance documents
148	
149	**What**: introduce versioned change history on tables holding legally-meaningful data:
150	- `Will`, `Bequest`, `BequestAllocation`, `WillResidueAllocation`
151	- `Asset` and all 12 delegated children
152	- `Marriage`, `Parentage`, `Guardianship`, `WillExecutor`, `WillWitness`
153	- `Trust` and role joins (when Trust epic activates)
154	
155	Likely implementation: `paper_trail` gem with `has_paper_trail` on each model. Stores a `versions` table with full row snapshots + who/when changes happened.
156	
157	**Why**: estate-planning documents are legal artefacts. "Bob says I left him 20% but the will shows 10% — what changed and when?" must have a clean answer for any user complaint, dispute, or legal proceeding. Without an audit trail this becomes unanswerable.
158	
159	**Effort**: half-day setup (gem add, migration, decorate models). Plus retro-fill question: do we backfill versions for rows created before the audit ships, or accept that pre-audit rows have no history? Recommendation: accept no-history for pre-audit rows, document that.
160	
161	**Origin**: ERD CTO review 2026-04-27. Mandatory before public launch; not blocking for internal testing.
162	
163	---
164	
165	## Cascade-delete integration test (GDPR right-to-erasure readiness)
166	
167	**What**: one Capybara/RSpec integration test that:
168	1. Builds a fully-populated `User` (will-maker `Person` + spouse + N children + `Marriage` + N `Parentage` + `Will` + `Bequest` + `BequestAllocation` + N `Asset` + delegated asset rows + `AssetPhoto` Active Storage attachments + `Invitation`s sent + `BeneficiaryGroup`s + `Trust` + role joins + `Business`).
169	2. Calls `User.destroy`.
170	3. Asserts every domain table has zero rows referencing that user.
171	4. Asserts Active Storage blobs in S3/disk are removed.
172	5. Asserts polymorphic associations (`BequestAllocation`, `WillResidueAllocation`) cleaned.
173	
174	**Why**: GDPR Article 17 right-to-erasure mandates complete deletion on request. Without this test, a single missing `dependent: :destroy` on a model association leaves dangling PII rows after a "delete my account" request — direct GDPR violation. Easy to break with one sloppy migration.
175	
176	**Effort**: 1 day. Plus fixing whatever the test surfaces (likely 1-2 missing cascade-delete declarations).
177	
178	**Origin**: ERD CTO review 2026-04-27, deferred from Epic 2 v1 per Elon scope challenge — internal testers won't trigger right-to-erasure, so it's launch readiness, not Epic 2 finish.
179	
180	---
181	
182	## Soft-delete consistency (`paranoia` or `deleted_at` everywhere)
183	
184	**What**: introduce a uniform soft-delete pattern across all domain tables, replacing the current mix of hard-delete and ad-hoc `is_active` flags.
185	
186	Likely implementation: `paranoia` gem (or hand-rolled `acts_as_paranoid` equivalent), adds `deleted_at` column to every domain table, default scope excludes soft-deleted rows, hard-delete remains available via `.really_destroy!`.
187	
188	Tables affected: `Person`, `Marriage`, `Parentage`, `Will`, `WillExecutor`, `WillWitness`, `Guardianship`, `Invitation`, `Asset`, all 12 delegate types, `Bequest`, `BequestAllocation`, `WillResidueAllocation`, `BeneficiaryGroup`, `Trust`, all role joins, `Business`. Auth tables (`User`, `Session`, `ApiSession`, `Device`) and `OnboardingSession` continue to hard-delete.
189	
190	**Why**: estate documents change over time; users delete a beneficiary, the next month want to undo. Soft-delete + 30-day retention beats forensic data recovery. Plus: GDPR right-to-erasure overrides soft-delete with explicit hard-delete on user request — soft-delete doesn't violate GDPR if hard-delete is available.
191	
192	**Effort**: 1 day (gem add, per-table migration adding `deleted_at`, model decoration, default-scope tests).
193	
194	**Origin**: ERD CTO review 2026-04-27. Particularly valuable for early-release / private-beta where users will make mistakes and want undo.
195
